danger_signals.visual_form: Animated hostile creatures (Beezos, Albatoss, Birdo, Mouser, etc.) with distinct sprite designs
danger_signals.meaning: Direct physical threat; enemies can damage player health or must be neutralized by throwing objects

danger_signals.visual_form: Quicksand in desert worlds, slippery ice surfaces in snowy areas, bottomless pits at screen edges
danger_signals.meaning: Death or health loss through world interaction rather than enemies

danger_signals.visual_form: Visual heart meter that depletes; character loses all health when meter empties
danger_signals.meaning: Imminent loss of life; character is vulnerable to any further damage

danger_signals.visual_form: Screen edges, gaps between platforms, open vertical spaces
danger_signals.meaning: Instant life loss if character falls off visible play area

danger_signals.visual_form: Large frog sprite at end of World 7, distinctly larger and more complex than standard enemies
danger_signals.meaning: Ultimate threat; final obstacle to completing the dream world challenge

safety_signals.visual_form: Visible ground tiles, ledges, and platforms that characters stand upon
safety_signals.meaning: Safe zone; no damage occurs while standing on solid surface

safety_signals.visual_form: Heart sprites that appear after defeating enemy groups
safety_signals.meaning: Restores character health; indicates safe moment to collect

safety_signals.visual_form: Mushroom item found in Sub-space areas
Distinct door graphics that lead to new rooms or Sub-space
safety_signals.meaning: Transition point; safe passage to new area, potential for shortcuts via warps

safety_signals.visual_form: Jar sprites that characters can enter
safety_signals.meaning: Access to Sub-space or potential warp locations

safety_signals.visual_form: Potion item in main areas that opens temporary Sub-space
safety_signals.meaning: Safe access to resource collection area with coin and mushroom rewards

faction_indicators.faction: Player characters (Mario, Luigi, Toad, Princess Toadstool)
faction_indicators.color_identity: Red/Green color schemes matching Super Mario Bros. palette
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Playable character chosen at level start; visible as protagonist sprite throughout level

faction_indicators.color_identity: Varied enemy colors; Wart is large green frog
faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Distinct enemy sprite designs (Beezos, Albatoss, Birdo with eggs, Mouser, Fryguy)
faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Each enemy type has unique animation and attack pattern visuals
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Enemies placed throughout levels in predictable patterns; different enemy types dominate different worlds

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: NPCs and neutral creatures visually distinct from threats
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Referenced in narrative context; world architecture and theming (desert, snow, forest) represents Subcon regions

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Full color sprite animation, standard game speed, normal enemy behavior patterns
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Enemies patrol set routes or follow attack patterns; player can move freely and interact with environment

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Screen inverts or reflects; becomes unscrollable bounded area; coins and mushrooms appear as collectibles
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Enemies do not appear in Sub-space; player can freely collect resources; jars in Sub-space provide warp shortcuts to later worlds

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Heart meter shows single remaining health point; character sprite may flash or show vulnerability state
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Any enemy contact or hazard causes immediate game loss; increased urgency to avoid all threats

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Large distinct boss sprite (Birdo, Mouser, Wart, etc.); arena typically cleared of regular enemies
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Boss follows scripted attack patterns (e.g., Birdo throws eggs at timed intervals); player must learn pattern and counter with thrown objects

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Flashing sprite effect indicating temporary invulnerability; visual feedback of power state
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Player takes no damage from enemies or hazards; can pass through threats safely

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Visual indicator or sprite change showing time-frozen state
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: All enemies freeze in place temporarily; player can move freely and avoid threats

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Each of 7 worlds has distinct visual theme (desert with quicksand, snowy areas, forest, etc.)
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Environmental hazards match theme; enemy types vary by world; difficulty and level layout evolves across progression

environmental_storytelling.example: Desert worlds introduce quicksand hazard visually; snowy worlds show ice and slippery surfaces; visual palette changes communicate world identity
Levels scroll vertically and horizontally (unlike original Mario), rooms connect via doors and ladders, jars as portals—visual communication that this is dreamland Subcon, not realistic space
Each world culminates in boss encounter; boss sprite design and arena layout communicate challenge escalation and faction presence (Wart's minions)
Vegetables pulled from ground, mushroom blocks, potions visually signal collectibles; hearts float up after enemy defeats showing reward feedback
Four playable characters with different silhouettes and color schemes; player's choice of character (Mario, Luigi, Toad, Princess) communicated visually before level starts
Potion triggers visual inversion/reflection of world; coins and mushrooms appear in this altered space; visual language communicates alternate dimension access

entity_behavioral_tells.behavior: Runs faster than normal (43 speed vs 36 speed); visual indication of enhanced capability when holding mushroom blocks or heavy items
Throws eggs at timed intervals; player can stand on eggs as platforms or throw them back; visual egg projectiles communicate attack pattern
Follows scripted movement and attack pattern; can be defeated by hitting with thrown objects; visual behavior tells player learning curve required
Fly in set patterns; can be jumped over or thrown at; visual flight pattern communicates threat level and avoidance strategy
Large bird carries bombs; visual distinctness communicates this is airborne threat requiring different approach than ground enemies
Splits into smaller flames when thrown; visual behavior change communicates multi-phase threat
Character sprite shows visible item carry state; movement speed may change based on item weight; visual feedback of mechanical impact
Freed from scrolling constraints; can collect coins and mushrooms safely; visual removal of enemies communicates resource-gathering phase

notes: Super Mario Bros. 2 uses dream world (Subcon) aesthetic to communicate that normal platforming rules are suspended. Vertical scrolling, multi-directional navigation, and object-throwing mechanics are visually reinforced through level design and enemy placement. The game lacks traditional faction warfare but communicates Wart's occupation of Subcon through enemy type distribution and boss encounters. Character selection screen establishes player agency before level begins, with visual distinction between abilities. Sub-space mechanic creates distinct visual and behavioral state—inverted colors, no enemies, accessible warps—communicating alternate dimension logic. Environmental theming (desert quicksand, snowy ice, etc.) both narrates world state and mechanically telegraphs hazards.
